question: Describe the multi-module NuScale power plant layout.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of conceptual design information (CDI), and notes that each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR while providing site-specific details as required by regulations.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report includes simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It lists the types of diagrams provided, references tables and figures for diagram legends, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0004.title: Containment Type
nuscale_ch01_0004.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and is maintained at a vacuum during normal operations, eliminating the need for insulation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) with natural circulation and no external piping for steam generators or pressurizer, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding approximately 570 MWe net output.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It lists the types of diagrams provided, references legends for interpreting symbols, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0003.title: Plant Location
nuscale_ch01_0003.summary: The section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It describes the modular NuScale Power Module (NPM), its key safety features, and the scalable plant design that allows up to twelve modules to operate within a single facility, highlighting unique safety and operational characteristics.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing operators to monitor and control multiple reactors using digital systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety functions. The MCR also provides comprehensive plant information and automated system monitoring for effective operation and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0043.title: Ultimate Heat Sink
nuscale_ch01_0043.summary: The Ultimate Heat Sink section describes a large, stainless steel-lined, reinforced concrete pool located below plant grade level, which serves as the final heat removal system for the plant. During normal operations, heat is removed from the pool via a cooling system and rejected to the atmosphere, while in the event of a total loss of AC power, decay heat is passively transferred to the pool, which has sufficient water inventory to ensure continued cooling. The spent fuel pool is cooled independently by its own system.

nuscale_ch01_0303.title: DSRS 9.1.3, Rev 0: Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup SystemII.5 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 63
nuscale_ch01_0303.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup System’s conformance with regulatory criteria, specifically addressing GDC 63. It states that the system fully conforms to the requirements of GDC 63, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and startup procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building and include spaces for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock, with provisions for safe transport and handling of fuel assemblies and reactor modules. These areas are designed to facilitate refueling, storage, and inspection activities while ensuring radiation protection for personnel.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout, while Section B offers detailed electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify and specify aspects of the general description in Section A.
Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout, while Section B specifies the type and design details of the containment vessel, which is one of the key systems described in Section A.
Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout and systems, while Section B offers detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify and specify the information summarized in Section A.
Section A provides a detailed summary of the plant's physical layout and systems, which elaborates on the general design description introduced in Section B.
Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout, while Section B specifies the detailed location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room within that overall layout.

Section A provides detailed electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify the design and layout of plant systems, which support the understanding of the containment vessel type and its integration described in Section B.

Section A and Section B are identical excerpts from the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, both providing references to electrical and instrumentation and control drawings that visually clarify the plant's design layout.
Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify aspects of the plant layout described in the general overview of Section B.

Section A provides detailed electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify the design and layout of plant areas, which includes the Main Control Room described in Section B, thus offering supporting visual context for the spatial and functional description in Section B.

Section A provides a general description of the containment vessel's type and design, while Section B offers detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify and specify the design information described in Section A.
Section A provides specific details about the containment vessel design, which is one of the structures described more generally in Section B's overview of the plant layout and safety analysis.

Section A describes the design and structural characteristics of the NuScale containment vessel, while Section B details the location and associated spaces of the main control room, both of which are components that together define aspects of the multi-module NuScale power plant layout.
Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the plant layout described in general terms in Section B's introduction and overview.
Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the plant layout, which includes the Main Control Room and its associated spaces described in Section B.

Section A provides a general introduction and overview of the NuScale plant design, while Section B specifies the detailed layout and associated spaces of the Main Control Room within that plant, connecting the general plant description to a specific facility component.
Section A provides specific details about the Central Utility Building and its functions, which are part of the overall facility described in general terms in Section B.

Section A describes specific plant structures and their functions, while Section B provides drawings and figures that visually clarify the layout and arrangement of such structures, connecting textual descriptions to visual representations.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building and its role in housing shared utility services for the plant, while Section B describes the containment vessel design for individual reactor modules, together providing complementary details about different structural components that make up the overall multi-module NuScale power plant layout.

Section A provides specific details about the ultimate heat sink and decay heat removal systems, which are components summarized in Section B's general plant description of the NuScale facility layout.

Section A describes the functional design and layout of the ultimate heat sink system, while Section B provides supporting electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify and detail the system's configuration as described in Section A.

Section A describes the ultimate heat sink and decay heat removal system, which relies on the reactor pool where the containment vessels (described in Section B) are immersed, thus connecting the cooling function to the physical containment layout.

Section A provides specific regulatory criteria and conformance details for the spent fuel pool cooling system, which is one of the plant systems broadly described in Section B's general overview of the NuScale power plant layout.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and conformance details for the spent fuel pool cooling system, while Section B provides the associated electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify the design layout referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses the spent fuel pool cooling system's design criteria and conformance, referencing the ultimate heat sink (UHS) as a safety-related system, while Section B describes the containment vessel's structural and pressure characteristics, both providing specific design features relevant to the overall NuScale multi-module plant layout.

Section A describes the ultimate heat sink system's design and function, while Section B references the ultimate heat sink as the seismic Category I supply system for spent fuel cooling, connecting them through the UHS's role in both reactor and spent fuel pool safety.
Section A provides detailed information about refueling operations for individual NuScale Power Modules, which is one of the specific systems summarized in Section B's general plant description.

Section A describes the physical process and independence of refueling operations for individual modules, while Section B provides drawings and figures that visually clarify the plant layout and component positions referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the movement of an individual NuScale Power Module (NPM) during refueling operations, which involves relocating the NPM (housed within its containment vessel) to the refueling area, while Section B specifies the design and structural characteristics of the containment vessel (CNV) that houses each NPM; thus, Section B provides the technical details for the containment referenced in Section A's operational description.

Section A provides specific details about the location and function of the fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas, which are components summarized in the overall facility description in Section B.

Section A describes the physical locations and functions of fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas, while Section B provides drawings and figures that visually clarify the layout and arrangement of electrical and I&C systems, supporting the understanding of the plant layout described in Section A.

Section A describes the physical location and function of fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas within the plant layout, while Section B details the design and classification of the containment vessel that houses the reactor modules, thus both sections provide complementary information about key structural components and their arrangement in the multi-module NuScale power plant layout.

Section A describes the process and independence of refueling operations for individual modules, while Section B specifies the physical locations and supporting infrastructure (such as pipe fittings and maintenance areas) that enable these operations within the multi-module plant layout.
